identity.canonical_title: Phase 1: Proof of Concept (Current Technology): μ_r ≈ 5 × 10^4

theory.canonical_statement: \textbf{Components:} \begin{itemize} \item Gyroscope: Ceramic hybrid bearings, $\omega = 5 \times 10^4$ RPM \item Dipoles: NdFeB permanent magnets, $B_r = 1.4$ T \item Boundary: Mu-metal sphere, $\mu_r \approx 5 \times 10^4$ \item Sensors: Commercial SQUID, $10^{-14}$ T sensitivity \end{itemize}
theory.technical_interpretation: Phase 1: Proof of Concept (Current Technology): μ_r ≈ 5 × 10^4 is a scaling / order-of-magnitude relation, written μ_r ≈ 5 × 10^4. It expresses a scaling or proportionality, capturing how the quantity grows with its parameters. It is built from r. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
